phases.description: Next.js 16 (App Router) + React 19 + Tailwind 3 scaffold, design tokens and global styles, and the single-page marketing surface (Hero, Pricing, HowItWorks, Conditions, Clinicians, FAQ, Nav, Footer). Complete.
phases.label: PHASE 1
phases.name: Foundation

phases.description: Compliance pages and content rules for LegitScript telehealth certification: /about, /privacy (HIPAA Privacy Policy + Notice of Privacy Practices), /terms (telehealth disclosure + refunds), FDA-status disclosures on Conditions cards, and the Apothem-to-Apoth rebrand with corporate-structure alignment (Apoth Health LLC + MD Integrations). Complete (PR #1 merged).
phases.label: PHASE 2
phases.name: LegitScript Compliance

phases.description: Resolve the unresolved DESIGN.md tokens (OKLCH clay/sage/cream palette values, display serif + humanist sans font selection), document the component primitives now that they exist in code, and run a WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility audit against the finalized palette and type scale.
phases.label: PHASE 3
phases.name: Design System
phases.summary: Finalize design tokens, document components, a11y audit

phases.description: Lean AWS serverless foundation for launch: Cognito, DynamoDB minimal app records, API Gateway/Lambda or Next server routes, Secrets Manager, CloudWatch logs/alarms, webhook idempotency, and SQS/DLQ only where retry durability is required. This phase explicitly excludes RDS/Postgres, Redis, App Runner, ECS workers, NAT gateways, and VPC endpoints unless a future architecture decision reopens them.
phases.label: PHASE 4
phases.name: Serverless Platform
phases.summary: Cognito, DynamoDB, Lambda/API routes, Secrets, CloudWatch, webhook idempotency

phases.description: Build out Apoth-owned patient authentication with Amazon Cognito: user pool/app client setup, sign-up/sign-in UI aligned to the design system, protected product routes, and minimal patient profile/linkage records in DynamoDB.
phases.label: PHASE 4
phases.name: Auth Flow

phases.description: Replace the /get-started stub with the real Cognito-gated patient intake flow: route structure, error/404/loading routes, state-availability logic, lightweight eligibility screening, MDI-provided questionnaire UI, submit-to-MDI handoff, and no local questionnaire-answer retention after successful submission.
phases.label: PHASE 5
phases.name: Intake Flow

phases.description: Stripe payment processor integration under the thin-PHI boundary: account and SDK setup, opaque non-PHI metadata policy, payment-method/checkout flow that cannot charge before the selected MDI clinical approval event, webhook handling with DynamoDB idempotency, and refunds/cancellation handling matching the Terms matrix.
phases.label: PHASE 6
phases.name: Payments

phases.description: The clinical system-of-record integration: MDI sandbox/event catalog, OAuth client-credentials token handling without Redis, single-entrypoint HTTP client, patient + case creation, MDI questionnaire submission with no local answer persistence, verified/idempotent webhooks, minimal case-status mirroring, embedded workflow URL helpers, patient dashboard data from MDI where feasible, maintenance handling, and scheduled Lambda reconciliation.
phases.label: PHASE 8
phases.name: MDIntegrations
phases.summary: MDI client + 40+ webhooks + dashboard + embedded URLs + reconciler

phases.description: 503A compounding pharmacy partner selection, BAA execution, and validation that fulfillment events flow through MDI's e-prescribe passthrough. Direct pharmacy API integration is out of launch scope unless a concrete partner gap reintroduces it.
phases.label: PHASE 10
phases.name: Pharmacy Fulfillment
phases.summary: Pharmacy partner BAA + validate MDI passthrough

phases.description: Real data and external sign-off required before LegitScript submission (deprioritized — mostly waiting on legal/corporate input): healthcare attorney review of /privacy and /terms, real contact info, mailing address, NPI, pharmacy partner disclosure, and finalized tiered pricing.
phases.label: PHASE 7
phases.name: Launch Blockers

phases.description: Launch hardening for the lean architecture: Stripe/MDI reconciliation, CloudWatch operational dashboards and alarms, staff evidence/support views over minimal records, dunning + cross-system cancellation sync, retention/deletion policy implementation, penetration test, and the LegitScript submission package with controls-evidence runbooks.
phases.label: PHASE 12
phases.name: Hardening & LegitScript Prep
phases.summary: Reconcilers, CloudWatch dashboards, LegitScript package, pen test, retention policy

phases.description: Production readiness and launch: SEO and metadata assets (robots, sitemap, favicon, OG image), analytics and cookie consent backing the privacy policy's cookie disclosure, production domain config with HTTPS/WHOIS, hosting decision finalization, and lean AWS serverless production deployment.
phases.label: PHASE 8
phases.name: Deploy
phases.summary: SEO assets, consent, domain, production deploy
